newsArray.title: Sri Lanka Cricket Enhances Coaching Team with New Appointments
newsArray.description: Sri Lanka Cricket has announced significant coaching appointments to bolster its National High Performance Center. Ryan van Niekerk will serve as the National Bowling Coach, while Jordan Gregory takes on the role of National Fielding and Spin Bowling Coach. These changes come in the wake of Gary Kirsten's appointment as head coach, following the resignation of Sanath Jayasuriya after a disappointing T20 World Cup. The new coaches are set to begin their roles on April 15, 2026, aiming to enhance the performance of Sri Lanka's national teams.

newsArray.title: AM Green India Golf Premier League Unveils Franchise Squads and $2 Billion Investment
newsArray.description: The AM Green India Golf Premier League (IGPL) has officially announced its franchise squads during a grand event in New Delhi. With a commitment of $2 billion towards developing golf tourism in India, the league aims to transform the country's golfing landscape. Cricket legend Yuvraj Singh and IGPL CEO Uttam Singh Mundy highlighted the significance of this initiative for player development and tourism. The league will feature 15 events, including international venues, and promises an exciting team competition format. This ambitious project is set to enhance India's reputation as a premier destination for golf enthusiasts.
creator: narendra chaudhary

newsArray.title: IPL 2026: You just be positive, brave and continue playing good cricket, says Narine
newsArray.description: Kolkata, April 2 (IANS) Kolkata Knight Riders’ veteran off-spinner Sunil Narine has said momentum remains the decisive factor in T20 cricket, adding that one has to be positive, brave and continue playing good cricket, as the franchise aims to get off the mark in IPL 2026 points table. Three-time champions KKR got into the clash on the back of a six-wicket defeat to the Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium on Sunday, despite posting 220 on board. "When you have the fans backing you and backing the entire team, it gives a little more to go there. IPL is about clicking at the right time, having the senior guys work with the youngsters, and obviously getting that momentum. “Momentum is everything in T20 cricket. You could get it and lose it as quickly as possible. So, you just be positive, be brave and continue playing good cricket," said Narine in a pre-game chat with the broadcasters, ahead of KKR facing Sunrisers Hyderabad at the Eden Gardens. The match also marks 200th IPL appearance for KKR skipper Ajinkya Rahane, with him becoming just the 11th player to achieve the feat. Narine added that younger players entering the league are better equipped than before to excel in the competition. "Nowadays, guys are coming to play a lot of T20 cricket. So, it is much easier than before. Guys are keen, and they don't take anything for granted. They know how hard IPL is, so they always put their best foot forward," he said. On his role in the side, Narine said he was ready to adapt to team requirements, especially with the bat. "I am someone who is whatever the team needs. If it's at the bottom of the innings, top of the innings, it's basically whatever the team requires and what plans are there," he added. --IANS nr/bsk/
creator: the hawk

newsArray.description: In view of the upcoming Indian Premier League (IPL) T20 match scheduled in Bengaluru on April 5, Sunday, the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited (BMRCL) has announced an extension of metro services to facilitate late-night travel for cricket fans and commuters.
